summary: Cleaned up Jigglypuff/Purin motion-state definitions by replacing raw motion flag and move-id hex constants in `ftPr_Init_MotionStateTable` with named `FtStateChange`, `FtMoveId`, and new `ftPr_MF_*` composed constants. The PR also added the needed forward includes and introduced a Purin-specific `ftPr_MotionFlags` enum in `forward.h`. Large inline-asm hunks appear to be formatting-only numeric operand normalization, with instruction comments unchanged in the excerpt. There was no PR body or review discussion, so intent beyond code-quality cleanup is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Added `#include "ft/forward.h"` to `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/forward.h` so Purin forward declarations can refer to shared fighter state-change constants.

Added a new `typedef enum ftPr_MotionFlags` containing composed Purin motion flag groups such as `ftPr_MF_Special`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialN`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirN`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialHi`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirHi`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialLw`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirLw`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialS`, and charged neutral-special variants.
Updated `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_Init.c` to include local `forward.h` and `ftMars/forward.h` in addition to `ft/forward.h`.

Replaced raw `MotionState` flag values such as `0`, `0x00340211`, `0x003C0211`, `0x00340611`, `0x00340013`, `0x00340413`, `0x00340014`, and `0x00340414` with named constants including `FtStateChange_None`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialN`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialNCharged`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirN`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialHi`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirHi`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialLw`, and `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirLw`.

Replaced raw move-id upper-byte constants such as `0x01000000`, `0x12000000`, `0x13000000`, `0x14000000`, and `0x15000000` with expressions like `FtMoveId_Unk01 << 24`, `FtMoveId_SpecialN << 24`, `FtMoveId_SpecialS << 24`, `FtMoveId_SpecialHi << 24`, and `FtMoveId_SpecialLw << 24`.

Touched many inline-asm hunks in `ftPr_Init.c`; the shown changes convert operands such as `-0x18`, `0x14`, `0x2c`, `0x2340`, and bitfield operands like `0x1b`/`0x1f` into decimal forms such as `-24`, `20`, `44`, `9024`, `27`, and `31`, while the embedded machine-code comments remain unchanged.
smart_moves: Factored recurring motion-state flag bitmasks into named, composable Purin-specific enum values instead of repeating opaque hex literals throughout the motion table.
Built air-state flag variants from their grounded equivalents plus `FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol`, making the ground/air relationship explicit and reducing risk of inconsistent future edits.
Separated the state-change flag field from the move-id field by using `ftPr_MF_*` for flags and `FtMoveId_* << 24` for move IDs, which documents the structure of each `MotionState` row.
Kept the change narrowly scoped to two Purin files and avoided renaming the motion callbacks themselves, limiting merge risk while improving readability.
Preserved matching-sensitive inline assembly at the byte-comment level in the excerpt; numeric spelling changed, but the instruction comments and labels indicate no intended behavior change.
decomp_lessons: For fighter motion tables, prefer named `FtStateChange_*` and `FtMoveId_* << 24` expressions over raw packed hex constants once the bit meanings are known.
When several action states share most flags, introduce a character-local motion-flags enum and compose variants from base masks instead of duplicating literals.
Air versions of special-move states may share grounded special flags plus air-specific preservation/skip bits; encoding that relationship directly makes table audits easier.

Forward headers are a reasonable home for character-local enums that are needed by init tables and other character files, but they must include the shared forward headers that define the referenced enum constants.

Large cleanup PRs can include formatting-only inline-asm churn; future reviewers should distinguish semantic table cleanup from assembler operand spelling changes by checking that machine-code comments stay stable.
naming_conventions: Character prefix used for Purin/Jigglypuff constants: `ftPr_`.

Purin motion flag members use `ftPr_MF_` plus action family, e.g. `ftPr_MF_SpecialN`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirN`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialHi`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirHi`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialLw`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirLw`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialS`, `ftPr_MF_SpecialAirS`.
Move IDs are expressed as shared `FtMoveId_*` values shifted into the high byte with `<< 24`, e.g. `FtMoveId_SpecialN << 24`.
State-change flags use the shared `FtStateChange_*` names and character-local aliases for repeated combinations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Raw motion-table constants were replaced with named expressions that should compile to the same packed values, improving source clarity without changing table layout.

Inline asm numeric operands in the excerpt were rewritten from hexadecimal to decimal, e.g. stack frame sizes and structure offsets, while machine-code annotations such as `94 21 FF E8`, `93 E1 00 14`, and labels remained unchanged.
The cleanup demonstrates a safe matching tactic: change symbolic names around data-table constants while keeping function pointers, row order, and assembly byte comments stable.
No evidence in the PR slice shows new decompilation of the inline asm functions; the visible asm changes appear textual/formatting rather than behavioral.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR dump.
The PR had no body text; conclusions about intent are based on the title, code-quality label, and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter `*_Init_MotionStateTable` files still using raw `0x00...` state-change flag constants.
Search for other character-local `*_MotionFlags` enums to align naming and composition patterns with `ftPr_MF_*`.
Audit `FtMoveId_* << 24` usage across fighter init tables for remaining raw move-id high-byte constants.
Check whether `ftPr_SpecialAirNCharged` should follow the `ftPr_MF_*` naming pattern, since it appears in the enum as a flag constant without the `MF` infix.
Identify inline-asm hunks where only numeric base changed from hex to decimal, to avoid treating them as semantic decomp progress.
